steps.caption: Trajanic Rome: Latin and imperial administration at a Mediterranean zenith — use Compare to layer faiths.
High Abbasid Caliphate: Arabic as a prestige language across a vast Islamicate sphere.
Mongol moment: steppe connectivity linking Beijing to the Black Sea — temporal shading shows the schematic maximum.
British imperial zenith (schematic boxes): English in law and education alongside indigenous language ecologies.

journeys.title: Americas — deep time to contact
journeys.subtitle: Archaeology and empire in the Western Hemisphere

steps.caption: Norte Chico: among the earliest monument-building societies in the hemisphere (illustrative coastal box).
Classic Maya Lowlands: hieroglyphic literacy and city-states — scrub the timeline to see polygons appear/disappear.
Inka Tawantinsuyu: Quechua imperial networks along the Andes — pair with temporal linguistic shading.
Iberian colonial South America: schematic viceroyalty-era boxes — illustrative, not treaty lines.
Third Dynasty Ur: cuneiform bureaucracy in a Sumerian–Akkadian matrix — Mesopotamian urban core.
Qin unification: script and standardization on the Chinese ecumene — temporal box over the Qin core.
Gupta-period Sanskrit culture: court epics and inscriptional networks across northern India.
Jump to the present: culture anchors and global ecumene — click empty map for the Field notebook.
